pairs.question: Who was Alexander Megas Komnenos and what was his role in the Empire of Trebizond?
pairs.answer: Alexander Megas Komnenos was a co-emperor of the Empire of Trebizond from approximately 1451 to 1459, ruling alongside his elder brother John IV Megas Komnenos. He was the second son of Emperor Alexios IV Megas Komnenos and initially designated as heir before being exiled following his brother John's return and seizure of the throne.

pairs.question: How did Alexander Megas Komnenos attempt to reclaim the throne of Trebizond?
pairs.answer: Alexander tried to regain the Trapezuntine throne by seeking Byzantine and Genoese support during his exile in Constantinople. He also married Maria Gattilusio, daughter of the lord of Lesbos, to secure additional allies against his brother John.

pairs.question: What was the relationship between Alexander Megas Komnenos and John VIII Palaiologos?
pairs.answer: Alexander was the brother-in-law of Byzantine Emperor John VIII Palaiologos, as his sister Maria had married the emperor. This relationship allowed Alexander to live in Constantinople during his exile.

pairs.question: Why did John IV Megas Komnenos invite Alexander back to Trebizond as co-emperor?
pairs.answer: John likely invited Alexander back and named him co-emperor because he had no sons of his own and distrusted their other brother, David. This reconciliation occurred around 1451.

pairs.question: What happened to Alexander Megas Komnenos' son after his death?
pairs.answer: Alexander predeceased his brother John in approximately 1459. His son, Alexios V Megas Komnenos, briefly succeeded John as emperor of Trebizond in 1460.

pairs.question: Describe the dynastic conflict between Alexander and John IV Megas Komnenos.
pairs.answer: The conflict began when John rebelled against their father, Alexios IV, in approximately 1426, leading to John's exile. Later, John returned, killed Alexios IV, and forced Alexander into exile. The brothers reconciled in the 1450s, with Alexander becoming co-emperor.

pairs.question: What was the outcome of Alexander Megas Komnenos' attempt to organize an expedition against John IV?
pairs.answer: Alexander's efforts to gain Byzantine and Genoese support for an expedition against John IV were largely unsuccessful. Despite marrying into the Gattilusio family for additional allies, he did not manage to depose his brother through force.
